message.content:
Let me systematically read the critical sections of vm.js to understand the syscall and memory model before building.

<command>
<keystrokes>grep -n 'readMemory\|writeMemory\|readMemoryWord\|writeMemoryWord\|readMemoryByte\|writeMemoryByte\|readMemoryHalf\|writeMemoryHalf' /app/vm.js | head -30</keystrokes>
<duration>0.1</duration>
</command>

<command>
<keystrokes>sed -n '420,500p' /app/vm.js</keystrokes>
<duration>0.1</duration>
</command>

<command>
<keystrokes>sed -n '1600,1700p' /app/vm.js</keystrokes>
<duration>0.1</duration>
</command>

{
  "analysis": "Need to understand the VM's memory model (endianness, how memory is stored) and syscall conventions before building. The VM reads ELF endianness and the parseSymbol function uses readUInt32LE, suggesting little-endian. Need to verify memory read/write methods and how syscall args are passed.",
  "plan": "Read the syscall handler, memory read/write methods, and ELF loader from vm.js to understand exact conventions. Then build with correct endianness and syscall interface.",
  "commands": [
    {"keystrokes": "grep -n 'readMemory\\|writeMemory\\|readMemoryWord\\|writeMemoryWord' /app/vm.js | head -30\n", "duration": 0.1},
    {"keystrokes": "sed -n '420,500p' /app/vm.js\n", "duration": 0.1},
    {"keystrokes": "sed -n '1600,1700p' /app/vm.js\n", "duration": 0.1}
  ]
}
